competitive_position.summary: Zycus is one of 3 shortlisted vendors competing against SAP Ariba (incumbent), unnamed peer vendors, and an EY proprietary Build option for a white-label tail-spend and ANA partnership. SAP Ariba's incumbency and EY's deep Ariba SI relationship create a high trust bar. The wedge is Merlin's AI-native autonomous negotiation, difficult to replicate in Build and ahead of Ariba's capabilities. The activation move is to make Merlin ANA the demo centerpiece with a live EY client use-case scenario, and provide white-label delivery proof points to de-risk the partnership vs Build.

champion_strength.source: Avoma 4 Feb 2026 call, SF Task 13 June 2026, SF Next_Step__c
champion_strength.summary: Stuart Ogilvie is a confirmed, active champion who initiated the partnership evaluation and is driving the 3-vendor selection internally. However, his championship is fragile: he exits EY 31 July 2026 (31 days), and the handover to Udipan is not secured (no Contact Role, no three-way call, no confirmation of Udipan's authority or sponsorship level). Stuart's Director-level title and the absence of any EY Partner or MD from the process suggest he lacks direct access to the Economic Buyer or commercial decision-maker. Without a secured handover and multi-threading into executive-level sponsorship, the deal collapses when Stuart leaves.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: no EB or Executive Sponsor role present
Avoma 4 Feb 2026 call: Stuart Ogilvie present, no executive-level attendees
SF Next_Step__c: references senior EY stakeholders for demo, none named
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified. A white-label partnership of this nature requires EY Partner or Managing Director-level approval to authorize go-to-market strategy, commercial structure (revenue share, client pricing model, implementation cost allocation), and multi-year commitment. The 150,000 Amount suggests internal investment or pilot-scale engagement, but no evidence in contact roles, calls, or fields names who controls that budget or the broader partnership decision. Stuart Ogilvie (champion, Director level) is the primary engagement point but lacks the authority to approve partnership commercials or GTM strategy. The 4 Feb 2026 call and 13 June 2026 task reference senior EY stakeholders for the pending demo, but none are named or confirmed as budget holders. The recommended move is to map the EY Partner or MD who owns the procurement consulting practice P&L and can approve the partnership business case, using Stuart or Gillian Bright as the pathway before Stuart's 31 July exit.

recommended_moves: Secure three-way handover call with champion Stuart Ogilvie and his successor Udipan before Stuart's 31 July exit. Use the call to confirm Udipan's sponsorship level, map the EY Partner or MD who will approve the partnership, and establish Udipan as the new champion with direct access to the Economic Buyer.

Schedule and execute the full demo of Merlin ANA with senior EY stakeholders within 14 days. Make the Merlin autonomous negotiation agent the centerpiece: run a live negotiation scenario on a tail-spend supplier case that mirrors an EY client use case, showing ROI and white-label UI configurability. Confirm stakeholder list (Gillian Bright, Colin Woods, Udipan, and the unnamed EY Partner or MD if identified) and use the demo to surface the Economic Buyer and post-demo decision timeline.

Provide white-label delivery proof points: share a case study, reference customer, or documented precedent of Zycus delivering a white-label multi-tenant SaaS partnership to de-risk EY's partnership decision and counter the Build option. If no precedent exists, prepare an architecture document and implementation plan showing multi-tenant governance, white-label branding configurability, and ERP-agnostic integration design.
